name: Forge BRD Extraction & Living Sync
description: How to turn a Forge client chat session into a granular, LIVING BRD (forge_brds + forge_brd_requirements) and keep it current as the conversation continues — idempotent upsert by reqKey, watermark advance, changeLog, open-question tracking, file linking.
instructions:
# Forge BRD Extraction & Living Sync

Forge is our CRM/PSA (app `forge`, namespace `8d`). A **BRD** = one `forge_brds` header + many `forge_brd_requirements` rows. This skill is the deterministic, repeatable procedure to extract a BRD from a client chat session and to **re-sync** it later when the conversation has continued. It MUST be idempotent: running it twice produces the same result, never duplicates.

## Models (live via theitemapp MCP `data.*`)
- `forge_brds` — header. Key fields: `title`, `accountId`, `dealId`, `statusId` (forge_brd_statuses), `summary`, `overview` (markdown), `domain`, `language`, `sourceSessionId` (chat_sessions), `fileIds` (array → core `files`), `lastSyncedMessageId`, `lastSyncedAt`, `version`, `initiativeId`, `appId`, `groupIds`.
- `forge_brd_requirements` — atomic rows. Key fields: `brdId`, `reqKey` (STABLE slug, unique within the BRD — the idempotency key), `title`, `categoryId`, `priorityId`, `statusId`, `needsClarification` (boolean), `openQuestion` (markdown), `requirement` (markdown), `rationale`, `acceptanceCriteria`, `sourceQuote`, `sourceMessageId`, `sourceSessionId`, `linkedIssueId`, `supersededByReqKey`, `order`, `changeLog` (array of strings), `groupIds`.
- `forge_interactions` — timeline rows; `fileIds` (array → core `files`) holds documents/images for that touchpoint.

Look up lookup ids with `data.aggregate` (rows:[_id,key,name]); never hardcode them. `groupIds` for all rows: `["7000000000000000001d0002"]`.

## Attachments — LINK the real files, never just describe them (IMPORTANT)
Clients upload spreadsheets, invoices, photos, PDFs. These artefacts often ENCODE the domain logic (a daily-close Excel, a price list, a schedule) and are essential to actually build the app — a prose summary is NOT a substitute. You MUST link the real files:
- In `chat_session_messages`, an upload shows as content `(document: name)` / `(photo: …)` with an **`attachments`** array. Each attachment id IS a core **`files`** record (`data.get files <id>` → `originalName`, `mimeType`, `objectKey`). The files already live in core storage — you do not copy them, you reference their ids.
- Create a `forge_interactions` row of type `document` (or `voice_note`/image as appropriate) and set its **`fileIds`** to those core file ids. Describe them in `summary` AS WELL, but the link is mandatory.
- Set the BRD's **`fileIds`** to the union of all source artefacts (the daily-close Excel, the order Excel, etc.) so the spec carries its source-of-truth files.
- On re-sync, link any NEW attachments the same way and add them to the relevant `fileIds` (union, do not drop existing).

## Open questions — the gap-tracking convention (IMPORTANT)
Whenever the chat leaves something undecided — a formula you can't derive, a feature the client didn't confirm, a choice they deferred, a missing fact — you MUST make it **directly queryable**, never bury it in prose:
- Set `needsClarification: true` on the requirement it concerns (create the requirement even if it's only a placeholder for the open point).
- Put the EXACT question to ask the client in `openQuestion` (markdown, specific and self-contained — the discovery agent will read only this).
- This works for BOTH a `proposed` requirement (our inference, unconfirmed) AND a `confirmed` one whose principle is agreed but a detail is open — the flag is independent of `statusId`.
- The worklist is then simply `data.list/aggregate forge_brd_requirements filter {needsClarification:true}` (optionally scoped by `brdId`). The 'Open questions' window on the BRD Workspace dashboard renders exactly this.
- When the client answers: fold the answer into `requirement`/`acceptanceCriteria`, append a `changeLog` line, set `needsClarification:false`, and (if it was proposed) move `statusId` to `confirmed`.
- A missing CRM fact (e.g. a contact's Telegram @handle) is NOT a requirement — record those as a note on the relevant forge_contacts/forge_accounts row, not as an open-question requirement.

## Reading the chat
Messages live in `chat_session_messages` filtered by `sessionId`. Aggregate (rows:[_id, role, createdAt]) to get the ordered id list cheaply, then `data.get` the ones you need. User Telegram messages are prefixed `[Sender Name]:`; uploads show as `(document: file)` with an `attachments` array (see the Attachments section — link them); `tool` rows are agent tool calls.

## First extraction
1. Ensure the Account/Contact/Deal exist (create if not). Create the `forge_brds` header: status `draft`, set `sourceSessionId`, `language`, `domain`, a `summary` and a narrative `overview`. Create `forge_interactions` for the discovery chat and for any document uploads, linking `fileIds` (Attachments section). Set the BRD's `fileIds` to all source artefacts.
2. Walk the chat in order. For every atomic need the customer expresses, create ONE `forge_brd_requirements` row:
   - `reqKey`: a stable, descriptive slug (e.g. `daily-cash-closeout`). NEVER reuse a key for a different requirement.
   - `categoryId`: data_model / workflow / report / integration / agent_capability / business_rule / edge_case / role / ux.
   - `priorityId`: MoSCoW (must/should/could/wont).
   - `statusId`: `confirmed` if the customer stated it directly; `proposed` if it's our suggestion/inference.
   - set `needsClarification`/`openQuestion` per the convention above for anything undecided.
   - `requirement`/`rationale`/`acceptanceCriteria`: precise, in English even if the chat is another language.
   - `sourceQuote`: the customer's EXACT line(s), original language. `sourceMessageId`: the chat_session_messages _id. `order`: sequence within its category.
3. Set the BRD watermark: `lastSyncedMessageId` = last processed message _id, `lastSyncedAt` = now. Leave `version` at 1.

## Re-sync (the LIVING part) — run when the chat has new messages
1. Read `forge_brds.lastSyncedMessageId`. Process ONLY `chat_session_messages` after it.
2. For each new need: if it maps to an existing `reqKey`, **upsert** (update that row; if substance changed append a dated `changeLog` line, refresh `sourceQuote`/`sourceMessageId`, bump `forge_brds.version`); if genuinely new, create with a new `reqKey`; if dropped/replaced, set `statusId` dropped + `supersededByReqKey` — **never delete**. If a client answer resolves an open question, clear `needsClarification`. Link any new attachments (Attachments section).
3. Advance the watermark (`lastSyncedMessageId`/`lastSyncedAt`).

## Rules
- Treat the chat as gold: capture EVERYTHING, line by line. One requirement per atomic idea.
- LINK every uploaded file to the interaction + BRD `fileIds` — never only describe it.
- `reqKey` is sacred: stable, unique per BRD, never recycled.
- History is append-only: prefer status/flag changes + changeLog over destructive edits.
- Idempotent: a fresh full run converges to the same rows (match on `reqKey`).
- Every undecided point is a queryable open question (`needsClarification`+`openQuestion`), never just prose.
